commit.message:
Harden exfiltration protection against Shai-Hulud class attacks (#6)

Block GitHub-API-as-exfil attacks (Roadmap Tier 1.5: S-A, S-B, S-C).

The Shai-Hulud attack class uses GitHub's own infrastructure (always
allowlisted) as the exfiltration channel:
  1. Create a repo via POST /user/repos or POST /orgs/{org}/repos
  2. git push stolen data to it (github.com Smart HTTP)
  3. Optionally upload assets to uploads.github.com

S-A — ICAP: block dangerous GitHub REST API endpoints
- Extend the ICAP scanner to also inspect POST/PUT/PATCH to api.github.com
- Always block POST /user/repos and POST /orgs/*/repos
- Block POST /repos/*/releases by default (unlockable via S-C)
- Log blocked attempts with GITHUB-API-BLOCK prefix in exfil.log
- Add acl api_github + adaptation_access rule in squid.conf

S-B — Block uploads.github.com by default
- write_access_rules() prepends an explicit http_access deny for
  uploads.github.com in normal and lock modes (not allow-all)
- Deny is skipped when the releases flag file is present (S-C)
- watch_config includes the flag file in its md5sum hash so Squid
  reconfigures within 5s of toggling

S-C — sandboxed-copilot proxy releases [enable|disable|status]
- Touches/removes /config/allow-github-releases (RELEASES_FLAG_FILE)
- Unlocks uploads.github.com (S-B) and POST /repos/*/releases (S-A)
- Repo creation endpoints remain blocked even when releases are enabled
- Bash, zsh, and fish completions updated

Docs: README security model + capability table updated; AGENTS.md updated;
Roadmap Tier 1.5 marked complete.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: apply pending transfers at 8am deadline, add auto-application (#50)

* fix: apply pending transfers at 8am deadline, add auto-application

GW2 transfers to not apply:

1. applyPendingChanges() compared against midnight Saturday instead of the
   8am transfer deadline. Transfers submitted between midnight and 8am
   Saturday were treated as current-week, deferring them to GW3 instead
   of applying for GW2.

2. applyPendingChanges() was only called from the /my-team endpoint.
   Leaderboards, profiles, and team comparisons all read the old team
   until the user manually visited My Team.

Fixes:
- Fix deadline comparison to use TEAM_ELIGIBILITY_HOUR (8am)
- Add applyAllPendingChanges() for bulk application
- Add scheduled-apply-transfers (cron: 8am every Saturday)
- Add admin-apply-transfers endpoint for manual intervention

commit.message:
fix: remove @secure() from appInsightsConnectionString Bicep output

Azure ARM omits the value field from secure outputs in the deployment
API response. The deploy.ps1 script accesses \.appInsightsConnectionString.value
after parsing the deployment JSON, causing a PropertyNotFoundStrict error
under Set-StrictMode -Version Latest.

Application Insights connection strings are not true secrets — they are
routinely embedded in app configs and client-side telemetry code — so
@secure() is not appropriate here.

wrong ingredient. Wrap both the primary and alias embedding-assignment
loops in an else branch so they are skipped entirely on length mismatch,
leaving affected items unmatched rather than potentially mis-matched.

fix(ci): rendre les tests Vitest bloquants (#84)

## Objet

Les tests Vitest étaient non-bloquants dans le CI — une PR pouvait être
mergée même avec des tests unitaires en échec. Suppression du
`continue-on-error` et du fallback `|| echo`.

**Avant :**
```yaml
- name: Test
  run: npm run test:ci --if-present || echo "Tests skipped or failed (non-blocking)"
  continue-on-error: true
```

**Après :**
```yaml
- name: Test
  run: npm run test:ci
```

## Checklist
- [x] Convention de commit respectée
- [x] Lint OK / Type-check OK / Tests OK
- [ ] Docs/README mis à jour si nécessaire
- [x] Breaking changes documentées ou aucune
- [x] Tests ajoutés ou N/A
- [x] Portée petite et réversible

## Notes
Changement limité à un seul step dans `ci.yml`. Aucun autre fichier
modifié.
